title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: monitOS provides statistical guidelines monitoring overall survival (OS) pivotal clinical trials indolent cancers — slow-progressing diseases (e.g. follicular lymphoma, CLL, low-grade glioma) experimental therapies often evaluated watch--wait standard--care control. core problem: indolent cancers, OS benefit hard prove reasonable timeframe, yet OS harm must obscured surrogate endpoints. package implements framework Fleming et al. (2024), defines sequence positivity thresholds — OS hazard ratio (HR) boundaries must met planned analysis give sufficient reassurance experimental treatment cause unacceptable harm. Key convention: HR < 1 indicates benefit; HR ≥ 1 indicates potential harm.

title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: Three clinically-determined ingredients drive every monitoring plan: Two error-rate parameters complete design:

title: Getting Started with monitOS
code: install.packages("monitOS") library(monitOS)

title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: Phase 3 randomised trial evaluates novel PI3Kδ inhibitor combined standard immunochemotherapy (R-CHOP) versus R-CHOP alone previously untreated Grade 1–2 follicular lymphoma — classic indolent B-cell malignancy long natural history. Randomisation: 1:1 (experimental : control) δ_null = 1.30 — 30% increase mortality risk deemed unacceptable population watch--wait safe alternative δ_alt = 0.80 — 20% reduction mortality risk plausible based mechanism Phase 2 PFS data 5 analyses planned 50, 80, 110, 140, 175 OS events γ_FA = 0.025, β_PA = 0.90
code: fl_trial <- bounds(   events     = c(50, 80, 110, 140, 175),   power_int  = 0.90,   # 90% power at primary analysis under δ_alt   falsepos   = 0.025,  # one-sided α at final analysis   hr_null    = 1.30,   # unacceptable OS harm threshold   hr_alt     = 0.80,   # plausible OS benefit   rand_ratio = 1       # 1:1 randomisation )  fl_trial$summary #>   Deaths OS HR threshold for positivity One-sided false positive error rate #> 1     50                          1.150                               0.332 #> 2     80                          1.065                               0.187 #> 3    110                          1.021                               0.103 #> 4    140                          0.993                               0.056 #> 5    175                          0.967                               0.025 #>   Level of 2-sided CI needed to rule out delta null #> 1                                                34 #> 2                                                63 #> 3                                                79 #> 4                                                89 #> 5                                                95 #>   Probability of meeting positivity threshold under delta alt #> 1                                                       0.900 #> 2                                                       0.900 #> 3                                                       0.900 #> 4                                                       0.900 #> 5                                                       0.895 #>   Posterior probability the true OS HR exceeds delta null given the data #> 1                                                                  0.332 #> 2                                                                  0.187 #> 3                                                                  0.103 #> 4                                                                  0.056 #> 5                                                                  0.025 #>   Predictive probability the OS HR estimate at Final Analysis does not exceed the positivity threshold #> 1                                                                                               23.425 #> 2                                                                                               27.725 #> 3                                                                                               31.742 #> 4                                                                                               35.834 #> 5                                                                                                   NA

title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: row corresponds one planned analysis. key columns : OS HR threshold positivity: observed OS HR analysis value, treatment considered reassuring (appear cause unacceptable harm). Notice thresholds tighten evidence accumulates — lenient ~1.13 50 events ~0.97 Final Analysis. One-sided false positive error rate: cumulative Type error spent analysis. Final Analysis exhausts full γ_FA = 0.025. Posterior probability HR ≥ δ_null: Bayesian probability true OS HR exceeds unacceptable threshold, given observed estimate equals positivity boundary — useful decision-support metric alongside threshold . Predictive probability (%): interim, probability Final Analysis also meet positivity threshold, assuming current trend continues. helps early stopping discussions.

previous_headings: Scenario 2 — Edge case: CLL Maintenance with Unequal Randomisation
title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: Phase 3 trial evaluates BCL-2 inhibitor maintenance strategy versus observation patients chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (CLL) achieved response first-line chemo-immunotherapy. scenario introduces two complications common real drug development: 2:1 randomisation — patients allocated experimental arm accelerate safety biomarker data collection. Marginal benefit sensitivity analysis — sponsor wishes understand monitoring performance pessimistic scenario drug provides modest 5% reduction mortality (HR = 0.95), well expected HR = 0.80 used size trial. δ_null = 1.25 — tighter margin Scenario 1 CLL patients already relatively good prognosis; even modest harm unacceptable δ_alt = 0.80 — 20% OS improvement anticipated based PFS phase 2 data 5 analyses 60, 95, 130, 165, 200 OS events hr_marg_benefit = 0.95 — marginal benefit scenario stress-test
code: cll_trial <- bounds(   events          = c(60, 95, 130, 165, 200),   power_int       = 0.90,   falsepos        = 0.025,   hr_null         = 1.25,   # tighter harm margin for CLL   hr_alt          = 0.80,   rand_ratio      = 2,      # 2:1 experimental : control   hr_marg_benefit = 0.95    # sensitivity: only 5% OS benefit )  cll_trial$summary #>   Deaths OS HR threshold for positivity One-sided false positive error rate #> 1     60                          1.136                               0.364 #> 2     95                          1.057                               0.221 #> 3    130                          1.015                               0.132 #> 4    165                          0.989                               0.078 #> 5    200                          0.932                               0.025 #>   Level of 2-sided CI needed to rule out delta null #> 1                                                27 #> 2                                                56 #> 3                                                74 #> 4                                                84 #> 5                                                95 #>   Probability of meeting positivity threshold under delta alt #> 1                                                       0.900 #> 2                                                       0.900 #> 3                                                       0.900 #> 4                                                       0.900 #> 5                                                       0.845 #>   Posterior probability the true OS HR exceeds delta null given the data #> 1                                                                  0.356 #> 2                                                                  0.207 #> 3                                                                  0.118 #> 4                                                                  0.066 #> 5                                                                  0.019 #>   Predictive probability the OS HR estimate at Final Analysis does not exceed the positivity threshold #> 1                                                                                               17.887 #> 2                                                                                               19.719 #> 3                                                                                               20.324 #> 4                                                                                               18.110 #> 5                                                                                                   NA #>   Probability of meeting positivity threshold under incremental benefit #> 1                                                                 0.743 #> 2                                                                 0.689 #> 3                                                                 0.640 #> 4                                                                 0.595 #> 5                                                                 0.448

previous_headings: Scenario 2 — Edge case: CLL Maintenance with Unequal Randomisation
what: Key observations for the edge case
title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: Effect unequal randomisation: 2:1 allocation reduces Fisher information per event compared 1:1, requiring slightly higher observed thresholds (room HR 1 still passing). reflected positivity thresholds fractionally higher Scenario 1 equivalent event counts. Marginal benefit column: rightmost column shows probability meeting positivity threshold true OS HR 0.95 — scenario drug barely works. Even pessimistic assumption monitoring thresholds likely met analyses, providing reassurance monitoring plan require large OS benefit conclude “unacceptable harm.”

title: Getting Started with monitOS
text: interactive version analyses, visit Shiny app — R installation required.
code: # Minimal call — all other arguments have sensible defaults bounds(events = c(60, 100, 150))  # Full call bounds(   events          = c(60, 89, 110, 131, 178),   power_int       = 0.90,   falsepos        = 0.025,   hr_null         = 1.30,   hr_alt          = 0.80,   rand_ratio      = 1,   hr_marg_benefit = NULL   # omit or set to a scalar HR for sensitivity analysis )

title: Interactive App (Shinylive)
text: monitOS Shiny app available Shinylive application runs entirely browser using WebAssembly — R installation server required. interactive app requires JavaScript. Please enable JavaScript browser visit standalone app directly.

title: Interactive App (Shinylive)
text: Shinylive runs Shiny applications entirely browser using WebAssembly via webR project. means: - server required — app runs device - installation needed — just open page - Privacy — inputs never leave browser app implements bounds() function available R package. programmatic access, install monitOS CRAN:
code: install.packages("monitOS") library(monitOS) bounds(   events = c(60, 89, 110, 131, 178),   power_int = 0.9,   falsepos = 0.025,   hr_null = 1.3,   hr_alt = 0.8,   rand_ratio = 1 )

title: Interactive App (Shinylive)
text: R monitOS installed, can also run full Shiny dashboard locally:
code: monitOS::run_app()

what: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: monitOS helps clinical teams define positivity thresholds monitoring overall survival (OS) pivotal trials indolent cancers. turns small set trial assumptions table operating characteristics can use interim final analyses: unacceptable detrimental effect OS want rule (hr_null) plausible beneficial effect OS still consider realistic (hr_alt) deaths expected analysis (events) framework described monitOS paper.

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: Install CRAN: Load package:
code: install.packages("monitOS") library(monitOS)

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: fastest way explore method interactive app. 🌐 Try online (Shinylive) — runs entirely browser via WebAssembly, installation required. 💻 Run locally R:
code: monitOS::run_app()

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: monitOS driven clinically meaningful choices. Key convention: OS HR < 1 indicates benefit.

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: minimal example creates positivity thresholds three planned analyses: look : OS HR threshold positivity tells largest observed OS HR still provides enough reassurance analysis. One-sided false positive error rate shows tolerated error stage. Level 2-sided CI needed rule delta null tells confidence level needed exclude unacceptable detriment.
code: library(monitOS)  plan <- bounds(   events = c(60, 110, 178),   power_int = 0.90,   falsepos = 0.025,   hr_null = 1.30,   hr_alt = 0.80 )  plan$summary

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: Interpretation: evidence accumulates, positivity threshold typically becomes stringent. Early analyses may tolerate observed OS HR slightly 1, final analysis usually requires threshold 1.
code: library(monitOS)  fl_trial <- bounds(   events = c(50, 80, 110, 140, 175),   power_int = 0.90,   falsepos = 0.025,   hr_null = 1.30,   hr_alt = 0.80,   rand_ratio = 1 )  fl_trial$summary

what: Example 2: Unequal randomization with sensitivity analysis
title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: adds incremental-benefit column showing often monitoring thresholds still met true OS effect modest main alternative.
code: library(monitOS)  cll_trial <- bounds(   events = c(60, 95, 130, 165, 200),   power_int = 0.90,   falsepos = 0.025,   hr_null = 1.25,   hr_alt = 0.80,   rand_ratio = 2,   hr_marg_benefit = 0.95 )  cll_trial$summary

title: Monitoring Overall Survival in Pivotal Trials in Indolent Cancers
text: Package vignette: https://opensource.nibr.com/monitOS/articles/monitOS.html Interactive app: https://opensource.nibr.com/monitOS/shinylive-app/ Function reference: Paper: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19466315.2024.2365648 Issues questions: https://github.com/Novartis/monitOS/issues
code: ?bounds ?run_app

title: Shiny app server function — app_server
text: Server-side logic monitOS Shiny application. Computes OS monitoring boundaries reactively based user inputs renders summary table positivity thresholds.

title: Shiny app server function — app_server
code: app_server(input, output, session)

title: Shiny app server function — app_server
text: input Shiny input object containing UI widget values. output Shiny output object rendering results. session Shiny session object updating UI elements.

title: Shiny app UI definition — app_ui
text: User interface monitOS Shiny application. Provides branded, guided workflow setting OS monitoring parameters reviewing resulting positivity thresholds.

title: Shiny app UI definition — app_ui
code: app_ui(request = NULL)

title: Shiny app UI definition — app_ui
text: request Shiny internal request object bookmarking support.

title: Bounds — bounds
text: OS monitoring guidelines proposed manuscript "Monitoring Overall Survival Pivotal Trials Indolent Cancers". Calculate thresholds positivity can used analysis judge whether emerging evidence effect treatment OS concerning . threshold positivity given analysis value observed hazard ratio must order provide sufficient reassurance effect OS reach selected unacceptable level detriment (margin hr_null). Terminology follows manuscript "Monitoring Overall Survival Pivotal Trials Indolent Cancers"

title: Bounds — bounds
code: bounds(   events,   power_int = 0.9,   falsepos = 0.025,   hr_null = 1.3,   hr_alt = 0.9,   rand_ratio = 1,   hr_marg_benefit = NULL )

title: Bounds — bounds
text: events Vector. Target number deaths analysis power_int Scalar. Marginal power required Primary Analysis true hazard ratio (HR) hr_alt. falsepos Scalar. Marginal one-sided false positive error rate prepared tolerate Final Analysis. Determines positivity threshold Final Analysis hr_null Scalar. unacceptably large detrimental effect treatment OS want rule (HR scale) hr_alt Scalar. Plausible clinically relevant beneficial effect treatment OS (HR scale) rand_ratio Integer. patients randomized k:1 experimental intervention control, rand_ratio inputted k. Example: patients randomized 1:1 experimental control, k=1. patients randomized 2:1 experimental control, k=2. hr_marg_benefit Scalar. may uncertain plausible beneficial effect treatment OS . User can enter (HR scale) function evaluate probability meet positivity threshold analysis HR. second OS benefit usually closer 1 hr_alt.

title: Bounds — bounds
text: List contains: lhr_null: Scalar, unacceptable OS log-HR, lhr_alt: Scalar, plausible clinically relevant log-HR, lhr_pos: Scalar, positivity thresholds log-HR estimates, summary: Dataframe, contains: OS HR threshold positivity, One sided false positive error rate, Level 2 sided CI needed rule hr_null, Probability meeting positivity threshold hr_alt, Positivity_Thres_Posterior: Pr(true OS HR >= minimum unacceptable OS HR | current data), Positivity_Thres_PredProb: Pr(OS HR estimate Final Analysis <= Final Analysis positivity threshold | current data)

title: Bounds — bounds
text: Monitoring guidelines assume hazard ratio (HR) can adequately summarize size benefits harms experimental intervention vs control overall survival (OS). Furthermore, guidelines assume OS HR < 1 consistent beneficial effect intervention OS (smaller OS HRs <1 indicate increased efficacy).

what: Function which calculates for k=1, ..., K, Pr(log-HR >= lhr_null | theta.hat.k = lhr_con.k) — calc_posterior

text: .e. posterior probability true OS log-hr exceeds minimum unacceptable OS log-HR given estimate log-hr analysis k equals lhr_con.k (.e. estimate equal stage k 'continuation threshold').

code: lhr_con <- c(0.2, 0.15, 0.1) events <- c(100, 200, 300) calc_predictive(lhr_con, events) #> [1] 0.2701457 0.2701457

title: Find Positivity Threshold — find_pos
text: function calculates positivity threshold based various criteria.

title: Find Positivity Threshold — find_pos
code: find_pos(pos_thld, events, rand_ratio, hr_null, hr_alt, which_crit, targ)

title: Find Positivity Threshold — find_pos
text: pos_thld Numeric. initial positivity threshold. events Numeric vector length 2. number events analysis. rand_ratio Numeric. randomization ratio. hr_null Numeric. hazard ratio null hypothesis. hr_alt Numeric. hazard ratio alternative hypothesis. which_crit Integer. criterion used finding positivity threshold: 1: False positive / False negative equals required value. 2: False positive / (False negative + False positive) equals required value. 3: False positive equals required value. 4: Predictive probability equals required value. targ Numeric. target value chosen criterion.

title: Find Positivity Threshold — find_pos
text: Numeric. calculated positivity threshold based specified criterion.

title: Find Positivity Threshold — find_pos
code: find_pos(   pos_thld = 1.5,   events = c(100, 200),   rand_ratio = 1,   hr_null = 1,   hr_alt = 1.5,   which_crit = 1,   targ = 0.05 ) #> [1] 1.907371

Target log HR calculate probability meeting positivity thresholds rand_ratio Integer. patients randomized k:1 experimental intervention control, rand_ratio inputted k. Example: patients randomized 1:1 experimental control, k=1. patients randomized 2:1 experimental control, k=2.

title: monitOS 0.2.0
text: Added Shinylive (WebAssembly) version Shiny app browser-use. Added new pkgdown article “Interactive App (Shinylive)” embedded app. Enhanced _pkgdown.yml custom navbar, article sections, reference grouping. Improved roxygen2 documentation app_server() app_ui(). Fixed app_ui() typo: = 12 corrected width = 12. Fixed incomplete paper URL Shiny app (now links published paper). Modernized calc_predictive() meeting_probs() use vapply() instead -loops. Added explicit return() calc_predictive(). Removed dead commented-code app_server() run_app(). Documented hardcoded false positive rate find_pos(). Aligned .lintr line width (80) air.toml formatter config. Added new tests calc_posterior(), calc_predictive(), meeting_probs(). Updated GitHub Actions: actions/checkout@v4, added shinylive export step. Added shinylive Suggests.

title: monitOS 0.1.6
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-17 Changed Licence MIT. Added spell checking package. Added github issues link BugReport website link URL `Description. Fix import issue shiny dependency. Fix bug returning inf values Shiny app. Added new badges packages

title: monitOS 0.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2024-03-21 Improved DESCRIPTION, added website URL, screenshot Shiny app Improved README Improved code style using styler Fixed bug deploying Shiny app (relative import error) Fixed package coverage focusing statistical tool (shiny app)

title: monitOS 0.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2024-03-05 Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Added Shiny section README.md file. Improved monitOS::run_app(): new layout, new descriptions, new use case. Created pkgdown public website.

title: monitOS 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2023-10-31 Initial CRAN submission.
